text:
There was once a little garden gnome named Gus who lived in a quiet village. Gus lived for two things: berry season and his daily walk.

Every autumn, his cheeks turned a deep purple from cherry blossoms, and every spring he could happily wander the gardens, humming happily to himself. His favorite spot was by the old apple tree.

One day, Gus heard a strange noise from the kitchen. He squinted his tiny eyes and listened. There were chuckles—no, not chuckles, giggles! He pricked up his nose.

Cautiously, Gus tiptoed toward the sink. There was Sally the squirrel! She was humming too.

"Golly," muttered Gus. "Should I say hello?"

"What? How?" Sally screeched.

"You laugh?"

"Um, yes? Why?"

"Well," Gus began cautiously, "what if I say something silly?"

Her giggle filled the air: "Oh honey!"

"Giggling about something silly?"

"N-not silly
